title: Add a workaround for a glibc bug when -lphtread precedes -lgpgme
body:
See detailed explanation inside the added file.

If you want to reproduce this, add an use of the `net` package somewhere early in the `main` package, and `sudo make check` (ensuring that your `fedora` image is Fedora 23).

comments.body: This sounds fine to me, the only stuff I don't like is the C linkage to compile. I would love to have a build tag to enable the whole gpg think, what do you think? Is it that bad to provide 2 kind of the same binaries with different functionalities? (I don't think so)

Um, that C linkage is the whole point of this workaround?!

I may miss the wider context, why is this undesirable?

To make this workaround optional with a build tag, all of the `signature` subpackage would have to be optional as well, and then I’m not sure what we would do about the CLI missing commands (and eventually essential commands if, as I hope, verification is enabled by default on every pull).

This can be dropped when glibc is fixed correct?

LGTM
We'll need to edit skopeo.spec in Fedora dist-git once we cut a new release of skopeo with this feature (and ensure deps are added in the spec and everything is fine)

> This can be dropped when glibc is fixed correct?

Yes.
